title: The mTOR–autophagy axis: why fasting works and how to time it right
excerpt: A deep look at the cellular mechanisms behind caloric restriction, the evidence on time-restricted eating windows, and what the latest trials actually show about longevity outcomes.

title: HRV as a recovery proxy: what it actually measures
excerpt: Heart rate variability is everywhere. Most people measure it wrong, interpret it worse, and act on it incorrectly. Here is what the science actually says.

title: Alpha-GPC vs CDP-choline: a side-by-side breakdown
excerpt: Two forms of choline. Very different pharmacokinetics. One is better for acute performance, the other for long-term neuroprotection. Here is how to choose.

title: Zone 2 cardio: the most underrated longevity tool
excerpt: Zone 2 training has a robust longevity evidence base that most people completely ignore in favour of HIIT. Here is what the research actually shows and how to do it correctly.

title: What your testosterone panel is not telling you
excerpt: Most men who get a testosterone test receive a total testosterone number and little else. This is often the least useful number on the panel. Here is what to actually test and why.

title: Cold exposure: separating dopamine reality from influencer hype
excerpt: Ice baths are everywhere. The science is more nuanced than the social media presence suggests. Here is what the research actually shows — and what it does not.

title: CGM for non-diabetics: signal or noise?
excerpt: Continuous glucose monitors have moved from diabetes management to biohacking. The question is whether the data they generate changes outcomes for metabolically healthy people.

title: Optimal sleep temperature: the evidence
excerpt: Core body temperature must drop by ~1°C for sleep initiation to occur. This is not optional physiology — it is the trigger. Bedroom temperature directly mediates this process.
